label: EU AI Act enters into force
description: The EU AI Act becomes law across all 27 member states. Prohibited AI practices (social scoring, real-time biometric surveillance) are immediately banned. A two-year compliance window begins for most regulated systems.

label: ISO 10218-1/2:2025 published — fenceless robotics standard
description: Updated ISO 10218-1 and 10218-2 safety standards for industrial robots published, enabling certification of cage-free high-speed robots. First compliant system (fenceless robot certified to ISO 13849-1 PL=d) operates safely at speeds up to 10 m/s alongside humans.

label: China imposes rare earth export controls
description: China MOFCOM Announcements 55–62 impose export controls on rare earths, permanent magnets, gallium, germanium, antimony, and graphite. China controls over 90% of refining stages for neodymium and dysprosium — the magnets inside every AI chip and robot motor.
useCasesBlocked: Western AI hardware scaling at current pace
_note: Controls suspended November 2025 under APEC truce; scheduled to expire or renegotiate November 2026.

label: US-China APEC techno-economic truce
description: At APEC, the US loosened chip export controls (allowing H200-equivalent chips to approved Chinese entities) and China suspended rare earth export restrictions. Truce valid until November 2026. Geopolitical analysts classify this as a tactical pause, not a strategic resolution.
useCasesUnlocked: H200-equivalent AI chips to approved Chinese entities
Western rare earth supply chain continuity (temporary)

label: EU AI Act: high-risk cognitive AI enforcement begins
description: The primary enforcement cliff of the EU AI Act. AI systems classified as high-risk (hiring, credit, medical, critical infrastructure) must be registered, audited, and conform to transparency and human oversight requirements. Non-compliant systems must be withdrawn from the EU market.
useCasesUnlocked: Compliant high-risk AI in EU (hiring, credit, medical, infrastructure)
useCasesBlocked: Non-certified high-risk AI deployment in EU workplaces
Unaudited AI hiring and credit scoring systems (EU)

label: US-China truce expiry — rare earth controls status uncertain
description: The November 2025 APEC truce expires. Rare earth export controls may resume, be extended, or be replaced by a longer-term agreement. The entire Western AI hardware supply chain is dependent on the outcome. Breaking Chinese rare earth dominance will take well over 5 years regardless.
_note: Outcome uncertain. Three scenarios: re-imposition of controls, rolling renewal, or formal agreement. Each has materially different implications for AI hardware costs and timelines.

label: EU AI Act: embedded robotics enforcement begins
description: Extension of EU AI Act enforcement to AI embedded in physical products including robots, vehicles, and medical devices. Humanoid robots deployed in EU workplaces must be certified. This is the gate through which the physical labour automation thesis must pass in Europe.
useCasesUnlocked: Compliant humanoid robots in EU workplaces

label: US SPEED Act: practical permitting reform takes effect
description: The SPEED Act (passed House December 2025) aimed to reduce US mining and infrastructure permitting from a weighted average of 8–9 years to 2–3 years. Practical effect projected 2028–2029 after judicial challenge stabilisation. Enables faster domestic rare earth mining and data centre construction.
useCasesUnlocked: Domestic rare earth mine development at reduced timescale
_note: Timeline is projected; judicial challenges could delay practical effect. Weighted average permitting time of 8–9 years from MACROENVIRONMENT-summary.md.

label: EU AI Act: legacy IT final compliance deadline
description: Final deadline for all legacy IT systems used by EU public bodies and regulated industries to achieve AI Act compliance. After this date, no exemptions remain. Marks the completion of the EU AI Act's full implementation across the economy.
useCasesUnlocked: Full AI deployment in EU public sector
useCasesBlocked: Non-compliant AI in any EU regulated sector
